title: FINRA Case 25-01728 — Carine Smith v. TIAA-CREF Individual & Institutional Ser
text:
Expungement Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-01728
Carine Smith
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Houston, Texas
TIAA-CREF Individual & Institutional Services, LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are appointed pursuant to Rule 13806 of
the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) to issue final, binding decisions. FINRA makes
available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by the SEC—but has no part in
deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Member
The expungement hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Carine Smith (“Claimant”): Ian J. McLoughlin, Esq., Shapiro Haber & Urmy LLP,
Boston, Massachusetts.
For Respondent TIAA-CREF Individual & Institutional Services, LLC (“Respondent”):
Christopher C. Coss, Esq., Coss & Momjian, LLP, Bala Cynwyd, Pennsylvania.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: August 19, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: August 19, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed on or about: October 8, 2025.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: October 16, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted a claim seeking expungement of customer dispute
information from registration records maintained by the Central Registration Depository (“CRD”).
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent did not oppose Claimant’s expungement request.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01728
Award Page 2 of 5
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant (CRD Number 4833492) requested expungement of all
references to Occurrence Number 2327462 from the CRD registration records.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent did not request any relief.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
On August 22, 2025, Claimant advised that the customer in Occurrence Number 2327462
(“Customer”) was served with a copy of the Statement of Claim within 10 days of filing the
Statement of Claim and provided proof of service.
On February 25, 2026, Claimant advised that the Customer was served with a copy of the
Statement of Answer, although not within 10 days of filing the Statement of Answer, and
Claimant provided an email sent to the Customer regarding prior transmission of the pleadings.
The Panel determined that Claimant filed the required proof of service for the Statement of
Claim and Statement of Answer on the Customer.
The Panel conducted a recorded hearing by videoconference on February 26, 2026, so the parties
could present oral argument and evidence on Claimant’s request for expungement. Claimant
appeared by videoconference at the expungement hearing. Respondent appeared at the
expungement hearing and, as stated in the Statement of Answer, did not oppose the request for
expungement.
The Customer appeared at the expungement hearing by videoconference and supported the
expungement request.
No authorized representative of a state securities regulator appeared at the expungement hearing.
The Panel noted Claimant’s representation that a prior arbitration panel or court has not
previously ruled on expungement of the same occurrence in the CRD.
The Panel noted that the dispute related to Occurrence Number 2327462 was not settled.
Therefore, there was no settlement documentation to review.
The Panel relied upon the following documentary or other evidence: Claimant's Exhibits 1, 3, 6,
and 8, which included the recording of the October 10, 2024, telephone call; the December 5,
2023, Customer Complaint e-mail; the December 27, 2023, TIAA letter to the Customer; all
Respondent’s documents produced related to the December 5, 2023, Customer Complaint; and
Claimant's testimony, which it found credible and uncontradicted.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01728
Award Page 3 of 5
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the expungement
hearing, and any post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of
the issues submitted for determination as follows:
The Panel awards the expungement of all references to Occurrence Number 2327462 from
registration records maintained by the CRD for Claimant Carine Fischer Smith (CRD Number
4833492) with the understanding that, pursuant to Rule 2080, Claimant Carine Fischer Smith
must obtain confirmation from a court of competent jurisdiction before the CRD will execute
the expungement directive.
Unless specifically waived in writing by FINRA, parties seeking judicial confirmation of an
arbitration award containing customer dispute expungement relief must name FINRA as an
additional party and serve FINRA with all appropriate documents.
Pursuant to Rule 13805 of the Code, the Panel has made the following affirmative findings of
fact:
The claim, allegation, or information is factually impossible or clearly erroneous; and
The registered person was not involved in the alleged investment-related sales practice
violation, forgery, theft, misappropriation, or conversion of funds.
The Panel has made the above findings based on the following reasons:
The Customer Complaint resulted from an October 16, 2024, telephone call
between B.J. of Respondent and the Customer. During that call, the Customer
instructed B.J. to transfer certain funds to certain other funds. B.J. (probably
unintentionally) had the specified funds transferred to funds other than the ones
requested by the Customer. The Customer discovered the error on December 5,
2024, and immediately advised her Respondent agent, Claimant, of the error.
Claimant immediately initiated procedures within Respondent to correct the funds
transfers and to fully cover losses suffered by the Customer in the interim period.
By December 22, 2024, the erroneous transfers were corrected and the
Customer's accounts were credited in full for losses incurred resulting from the
erroneous transfers. The undisputed evidence established beyond doubt that
Claimant not only had no involvement in the erroneous transfers but immediately
took steps that fully resolved the complaint as soon as it came to her attention. We
noted that the Customer appeared in the hearing and advised the Panel that she
fully supported expungement.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01728
Award Page 4 of 5
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 2,000.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,600.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,005.00
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
One (1) pre-hearing session with the Panel @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Pre-Hearing Conference: November 17, 2025 1 session
One (1) hearing session on expungement request @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Hearing: February 26, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 3,450.00
The Panel has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01728
Award Page 5 of 5
ARBITRATION PANEL
Michael S. Yauch - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Seth L. Finkel - Public Arbitrator
Jan Preece Gaddis - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
Michael S. Yauch 03/23/2026

March 24, 2026
